title: derris fordii
intro: Derris fordii, also known as the 'Buddha's Tree', is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Asia, particularly in Sri Lanka. It is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of rotenone, a natural insecticide, and other bioactive compounds. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, and is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The herbal preparations of Derris fordii often include extracts, decoctions, or infusions, which can be used to make teas, tinctures, and other remedies to harness its medicinal benefits.
